 {"id":"bd-db300.10.6","title":"J6: audit and reduce PageData ownership transitions, full-page normalization, and clone-heavy representation flips","description":"## Goal
Remove representation churn that turns ordinary page movement into clone-heavy work.

## Scope
- inventory clone->vec->arc or equivalent ownership flips in the hot path
- reduce unnecessary full-page normalization before dirtiness or mutation is known
- keep representation changes explicit and justified when they remain necessary
- add focused tests and measurements proving the hot path performs fewer ownership transitions and less byte copying

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the hot path performs fewer PageData ownership transitions and fewer unnecessary full-page normalizations
- tests cover correctness of the remaining conversion boundaries
- focused evidence shows lower memcpy/memmove/copy signatures on representative rows","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the hot path performs fewer PageData ownership transitions and fewer unnecessary full-page normalizations
- tests cover correctness of the remaining conversion boundaries
- focused evidence shows lower memcpy/memmove/copy signatures on representative rows","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add focused tests around remaining representation-conversion boundaries so ownership simplification does not silently corrupt aliasing or lifetime assumptions
- add evidence packs that report pagedata_transition_count, full_page_normalization_count, and staged_copy_bytes on representative rows
- tie any remaining expensive conversions to an explicit justification in the bead closeout","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:40.839040838Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:18.118250154Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-j","pagedata","pager","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:40.839040838Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:25.578635988Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:35.663600604Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2307,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.5 and O.6.
- This is the PageData ownership-transition / full-page-normalization reduction bead.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:41Z"},{"id":2458,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"TealStream","text":"PageData/baseline-tax note (2026-03-18):
- The prior instrumentation pass already landed PageData motion counters (`owned_passthrough`, resized copies, normalized payload bytes, zero-fill bytes) in the hot-path report pipeline. That work is the current measurement wedge for J6/J3.
- Fresh canonical c8 artifact capture with those counters is temporarily blocked by the `rch` 300s timeout on `release-perf` hot-profile replay, so there is not yet a new scored artifact pack tying PageData motion share to the current tree state.
- In parallel, I took a different bounded fixed-cost cut in `begin_concurrent.rs`: replace scan-based `release_all(txn_id)` with exact `release_set(...)` on the concurrent path when held-lock state is available. This is not a J6 change directly, but it helps separate MVCC coordination tax from PageData-motion tax so later J6 decisions are based on cleaner attribution rather than a blended microtransaction penalty.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:07:00Z"},{"id":2461,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"TealStream","text":"Progress update (2026-03-18, pass 2):
- I re-read the landed PageData motion instrumentation in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` and the corresponding hot-path report wiring in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs` / `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs`. I did not find a concrete logic bug in the counters themselves during this pass.
- Fresh-eyes clarification: the new PageData motion report is allocator/copy evidence, not a standalone wall-time partition. The markdown/report wording already notes that component shares may overlap, so I left the semantics intact.
- This lane still needs a fresh artifact pack to become decisive, but the ranking/reporting plumbing now exposes owned passthrough vs resized-copy outcomes and total normalized bytes, which is the right evidence surface for J6/J3 once the remote replay blocker is gone.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.7","title":"J7: simplify copy-heavy decode and cell-assembly kernels while reusing cursor and frame structures","description":"## Goal
Target the measured memcpy/memmove-heavy kernels rather than making generic promises about speed.

## Scope
- isolate one or more measured copy-heavy decode/cell-assembly kernels
- simplify them or replace them with reuse-friendly kernels grounded in evidence rather than cleverness for its own sake
- reuse cursor/frame structures where that reduces setup/teardown churn without confusing lifetime boundaries
- add unit and microbenchmark tests that prove semantic parity and reduced copy volume

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- one or more measured copy-heavy kernels are materially cheaper after the change
- reused cursor/frame structures preserve correctness and are covered by tests
- benchmarks show lower copy volume or better throughput on the rows that motivated the change","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- one or more measured copy-heavy kernels are materially cheaper after the change
- reused cursor/frame structures preserve correctness and are covered by tests
- benchmarks show lower copy volume or better throughput on the rows that motivated the change","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit or microbench tests for the exact decode/cell-assembly kernels being simplified, with semantic parity assertions and copy-volume reporting
- add e2e evidence showing the targeted workloads actually exercised the optimized kernel path
- emit structured counters for hot_kernel_path_selected and kernel_copy_bytes so attribution remains concrete","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:52.137856704Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:29:22.309299234Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","btree","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-j","decoding","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:52.137856704Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:45.778182684Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:56.221476593Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2308,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Primarily covers the measured-copy-reduction side of P5.6 and supports P5.9 by attacking setup/teardown and copy-heavy kernels directly.
- This bead exists because the gospel plan names reusable frames/VDBE fast paths, but the actual kernel-level copy churn also needed a first-class implementation target.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:49Z"},{"id":2469,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","author":"Codex","text":"In progress: narrowed the direct table-program execution path so runtime metadata is scoped per statement instead of rebuilding blanket insert/default/autoincrement inputs every execution. Simple VALUES/DEFAULT VALUES inserts now skip the connection-level legacy default-map pass, direct insert paths scope AUTOINCREMENT sequence lookup to the target root page only, and the hot-path profile artifacts now expose prepared-engine reuse plus connection-ceremony counters (prepared engine fresh/reuse, snapshot reuse/parks, schema/publication refreshes, sqlite_sequence fast-path vs scan fallback). Targeted core/e2e verification is running via rch.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:29:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.8","title":"J8: add VDBE fast paths, reusable execution frames, and safe direct read-path improvements where profitable","description":"## Goal
Shrink non-structural execution overhead in the statement engine and read path.

## Scope
- add evidence-driven VDBE fast paths or fused hot opcode handling where profitable and maintainable
- reuse execution frames/cursor scaffolding where safe
- evaluate safe direct pager-buffer fills, registered read buffers, optional mmap-like read paths, or equivalent read-path reductions for benchmarked environments without violating correctness or portability constraints
- add tests that prove semantic parity and logs that make fast-path selection visible

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- fast paths are evidence-driven and visibly exercised in representative workloads
- execution-frame reuse and read-path improvements preserve correctness under test
- representative c1 or mixed rows improve for reasons that can be traced back to the new fast paths","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- fast paths are evidence-driven and visibly exercised in representative workloads
- execution-frame reuse and read-path improvements preserve correctness under test
- representative c1 or mixed rows improve for reasons that can be traced back to the new fast paths","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for fast-path correctness, frame reuse safety, direct read-path boundary conditions, and any registered-buffer or mmap-like path boundary that is introduced
- add representative c1/mixed runs that emit vdbe_fast_path_selected, frame_reuse_hit, direct_read_path_used, registered_read_buffer_used, mmap_like_path_used, and fast_path_fallback_reason
- keep any fast path behind explicit observability so future regressions can be localized quickly
- optimize for users by preferring read-path improvements that remain understandable, optional where needed, and clearly bounded by environment/portability assumptions","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaSnow","owner":"FuchsiaSnow","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:02.906268102Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-19T05:12:15.233407283Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","db300-program","db300-track-j","read-path","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:02.906268102Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:06.622932299Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:16.942865773Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2309,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.6, P5.7, and O.6.
- This is the VDBE fast-path / reusable-frame / safe direct-read-path bead, including registered-buffer and optional mmap-like explorations where justified.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.10.9","title":"J9: harden perf-binary build hygiene so tracing, debug tax, and profile noise do not hide real wins","description":"## Goal
Take the free and low-risk wins in build/profile hygiene and make the performance binaries honest.

## Scope
- ensure release-perf or equivalent binaries exclude unnecessary tracing/debug/assert overhead on hot paths
- evaluate LTO/PGO, crate/module flattening for hot-path inlining, and related low-risk build/layout improvements where practical
- make perf-binary settings explicit and testable so future agents know which binary is authoritative for throughput work
- add artifact metadata proving which build hygiene knobs were active during measured runs

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- perf-binary settings are explicit, reproducible, and attached to artifacts
- avoidable debug/tracing tax is absent from the authoritative perf binary
- the change set produces measurable or at least defensible noise reduction in benchmark interpretation","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- perf-binary settings are explicit, reproducible, and attached to artifacts
- avoidable debug/tracing tax is absent from the authoritative perf binary
- the change set produces measurable or at least defensible noise reduction in benchmark interpretation","notes":"Planning note:
J9 should run in parallel with the main baseline-tax work once manifest/provenance fields are ready; it does not need to wait for the entire J1 truthing story.

Implementation proof requirements:
- define the authoritative perf binary/profile, the expected metadata fields proving which knobs were active, and the comparison procedure for before/after noise reduction
- record explicit structured metadata for tracing/debug settings, LTO/PGO state where applicable, crate/module-flattening or inlining-oriented layout changes where applicable, and other build-hygiene knobs so benchmark artifacts remain interpretable
- optimize for users by making the authoritative perf binary obvious and reproducible instead of something only the current operator remembers","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:13.568779171Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:22.315067397Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","build-system","db300-program","db300-track-j","perf-binaries","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:39:16.071689992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:13.568779171Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2310,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.8 and O.6.
- This is the build/profile-hygiene bead for LTO/PGO, tracing-debug tax removal, and hot-path inlining/layout clarity.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:51Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.11","title":"Overlay: 2026-03-23 benchmark-gap closure program for c1 fixed tax and persistent 8t/16t end-to-end gap","description":"This overlay is now anchored to two hard failures. First, the authoritative c1 rerun is still catastrophic, which means low-concurrency fixed per-operation cost remains unresolved. Second, the persistent 8t/16t benchmark is still red even after the old timeout-collapse signature largely disappeared, which means the dominant persistent cost now sits outside the currently measured commit-center buckets. The overlay must therefore prioritize closed latency accounting, root-cause ranking, and targeted fixes over more scorecard churn.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:20:57.810331334Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T20:44:56.572427750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","overlay-2026-03-23","perf","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:20:57.810331334Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:09.958455044Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T20:44:56.571252429Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:11.364607718Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:10.683607892Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:12.144780154Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:12.976527488Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","depends_on_id":"bd-hjkbr.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T20:44:55.376490183Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2533,"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","author":"codex","text":"## Why this overlay exists

This overlay captures the concrete 2026-03-23 local benchmark reality and turns it into an executable work graph. The intent is to eliminate any need to reconstruct the plan from session history or external markdown.

The baseline for this overlay is the local `release-perf` run on this machine, without `rch`, using the current code on 2026-03-23. The real-DB canonical matrix came back at roughly `0.584x` SQLite for MVCC and `0.451x` for forced single-writer on the 27 comparable cells. The most important split is by concurrency: both MVCC and single-writer were only about `0.209x` / `0.208x` of SQLite at `c1`, while MVCC improved to about `2.538x` at `c8` and single-writer only reached about `1.077x`. The synthetic persistent concurrent-writer benchmark was strong at `2t` and `4t` (`5.51x`, `3.68x`), near parity at `8t` (`0.98x`), and catastrophically red at `16t` (`0.18x`).

Those numbers imply two different diseases:
- Low concurrency is dominated by a fixed service-time tax that exists even when MVCC is not the differentiator.
- High concurrency, especially persistent `16t`, is dominated by queueing/tail/commit-path collapse rather than a simple average-service problem.

This overlay therefore splits the work into five lanes:
- `A7` refreshes the authoritative evidence packs around the exact red cells so optimization starts from measured reality.
- `D5` attacks the `c1` fixed tax in parser/prepare/schema-refresh/materialization/decode hot paths.
- `C8` attacks the persistent `8t/16t` collapse in the pager/WAL/group-commit path.
- `E8` is the evidence-gated advanced-design lane. It exists so we can use ideas from extreme optimization, alien-artifact design, and the graveyard without letting speculative cleverness outrun proof.
- `G9` upgrades the scorecard and gating surface so fake wins stop surviving aggregation, skew, or stale placeholder tests.

## Non-negotiable project goals carried by this overlay

The project goal is not merely to post a few green cells. The goal is to make FrankenSQLite a materially faster drop-in replacement than SQLite while preserving the entire point of the project: concurrent-writer mode stays on by default, correctness remains strict, corruption bugs stay dead, and compatibility stays strong enough that the faster engine is actually usable.

The practical reading of that goal for this overlay is:
- every important benchmark cell eventually needs to exceed `1.0x` SQLite with healthy margin,
- no optimization is allowed to buy speed by degrading crash safety, corruption freedom, isolation, or drop-in replacement behavior,
- no aggregate score is allowed to hide a red regime that users will actually feel,
- each material change must come with a before/after evidence ledger strong enough that future work can tell what really helped.

## Guidance for future agents

Do not treat this overlay as a license to jump straight to exotic architecture. The discipline here is profile first, prove behavior, change one primary lever at a time, and keep a conservative rollback-safe fallback for any new controller or concurrency primitive. Advanced cards are present because the user explicitly wants the smartest available options on the table, but the dependency graph intentionally places them after the measurement refresh and the first-order bottleneck work.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:30:18Z"},{"id":2543,"issue_id":"bd-db300.11","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: polish round 2

This overlay now encodes completion against the final evidence and verification leaves rather than treating the overlay root as if it were a direct implementation task. That change is intentional: `bv` and ready views should surface real work, not program-summary beads.

This pass also makes validation first-class. Every overlay-created bead now carries the `overlay-2026-03-23` label so scoped graph analysis sees the whole program, and the low-tax and commit-collapse lanes now have explicit unit-test, e2e-script, and detailed-logging beads instead of relying on implicit promises that generic verification work will materialize later.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.2","title":"Track B: eliminate avoidable blocking and synchronization","description":"## Goal
This track attacks the strongest current signal: excessive synchronization and blocking on the writer hot path. The c8 mixed_read_write path only utilized about half a CPU despite eight workers, and strace showed futex dominating traced syscall time. That means many-core hardware is being left idle because the engine still serializes or waits more than it should.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The blocking topology is explicitly mapped.
- Each remaining shared synchronization site has a justification or a removal plan.
- Writer hot-path waiting is materially reduced in follow-on work.","design":"- Inventory every lock, futex wait, sleep, yield, and serialized region touched by execute and commit.
- Remove or shard shared state that does not need to be globally synchronized.
- Promote wait-free, lock-free, or bounded-handoff publication strategies where correctness permits.","acceptance_criteria":"- The blocking topology is explicitly mapped.
- Each remaining shared synchronization site has a justification or a removal plan.
- Writer hot-path waiting is materially reduced in follow-on work.","notes":"One already confirmed example in this family was the JIT bookkeeping mutex on the VDBE execute path. Making the scaffold opt-in removed a real source of overhead and yielded about a 6.18% speedup, but the futex-heavy profile shows there is still much more to remove.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:46:17.901760674Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:29.920760062Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","busy","concurrency","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-b","hot-path","locking","mechanical-sympathy","parallelism","perf","track-b"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.2.1","title":"B1: map all writer hot-path locking, futex, sleep, and yield sites","description":"## Goal
Produce a concrete inventory of every mutex, futex wait, sleep, yield, serialized region, and shared-state choke point that the writer hot path touches from execute through commit. This is the bead that turns a vague 'too much blocking' complaint into a prioritized removal list with measured wait share.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The major waiting and synchronization sites on the hot path are listed with evidence.
- Each site has an owner, rough cost, and candidate removal/sharding direction.
- The inventory is specific enough to drive the follow-on synchronization-removal beads.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"- The major waiting and synchronization sites on the hot path are listed with evidence.
- Each site has an owner, rough cost, and candidate removal/sharding direction.
- The inventory is specific enough to drive the follow-on synchronization-removal beads.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"This bead should capture both CPU-side blocking (futex waits, yields, sleeps) and structural serialization such as overlarge critical sections around commit/publish work.

2026-03-10 CloudyCliff progress note:

Evidence gathered:
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/working/beads_bench_20260310/profiles/fsqlite_mvcc_c4_mixed_strace.txt`: futex = 60.42% of syscall time, `sched_yield` = 0.37%, `clock_nanosleep` = 0.24%, `fsync` = 0.16%. `wait4` is also large in this capture but appears harness/process-management-related, so I am not assigning it to the engine hot path.
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/working/beads_bench_20260310/profiles/current_fair_fsqlite_mvcc_c8_frankensqlite_beads_perfstat.txt`: `task-clock=3.665s` over `7.174s` elapsed => only `0.511 CPUs utilized` at c8. That is the strongest current signal that the writer path is still over-serialized.
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/working/beads_bench_20260310/results/retry_executor_c8_mvcc.md`: `frankensqlite_beads:c8` median `608 ops/s`, with `13-21` retries / aborts per 400 ops.
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/working/beads_bench_20260310/results/retry_executor_c8_single.md`: forced single-writer median `66 ops/s`, with `41-42` retries / aborts per 400 ops. This is much worse, but the same synchronization sites still show up.
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/working/beads_bench_20260310/profiles/fsqlite_mvcc_c4_mixed.perf.data`: the non-wait CPU remainder is allocator / copy / record-decode heavy (`_int_malloc` 11.2%, `__memmove_avx_unaligned_erms` 11.0%, `parse_record_into` 2.2%, `CellRef::parse` 1.6%, `MemTable` clone/drop visible). That belongs to Track D, not this bead, but it helps separate waiting from useful-but-expensive CPU work.

Writer hot-path synchronization inventory:
1. Global commit serialization guard.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`.
   - Evidence: `commit_write_mutex` field at `connection.rs:1595-1597`; held across `plan_concurrent_commit -> txn.commit -> advance_commit_clock -> finalize_concurrent_commit` at `connection.rs:9851-9874`.
   - Rough cost: P0 structural choke point. Every commit across shared connections queues behind one mutex before pager commit and finalize complete; aligns with c8 `0.511 CPUs utilized`.
   - Candidate direction: shrink the guarded region to true single-file ordering only (commit-seq assignment + durable marker append / final publish), and move FCW/SSI planning plus precomputed WAL work out of the guard.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.3.1`, `bd-db300.3.2`, `bd-db300.5.1`.
2. Global `ConcurrentRegistry` mutex on the ordinary MVCC read/write path.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` + `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`.
   - Evidence: shared field `connection.rs:1567-1571`; begin path locks it at `connection.rs:9437-9440`; commit planning/finalization lock it at `connection.rs:9831-9838`; page read/witness/write paths lock it at `engine.rs:738-751`, `engine.rs:783-799`, and `engine.rs:820-839`.
   - Rough cost: P0 structural choke point. The same mutex is touched on begin, every read witness, every dirty/write-set update, and the full SSI prepare/finalize path. This is a credible primary source of the futex-heavy profile.
   - Candidate direction: split per-session mutable state out of the global registry, make active-view/history reads read-mostly (RCU/seqlock/sharded snapshots), and stop taking the same mutex for every page-level witness update.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.2.2`, `bd-db300.2.3`, `bd-db300.5.1`, `bd-db300.5.5`.
3. Page-lock contention retry loop with explicit sleeps.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` + MVCC lock-table path.
   - Evidence: `SharedTxnPageIo::write_page` at `engine.rs:807-901`; on `MvccError::Busy` it sleeps with exponential backoff from `50us` to `5ms` at `engine.rs:855-881`.
   - Rough cost: direct wait site. This is the clearest source for the observed `clock_nanosleep` / yield behavior once a writer collides on a page lock.
   - Candidate direction: replace blind sleep/backoff with bounded handoff or waiter-queue semantics, and keep retry accounting separate from commit-serialization costs.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.2.4`.
4. `InProcessPageLockTable` shard mutexes plus finalize-time full-table release scan.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/core_types.rs` + `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs`.
   - Evidence: lock table is 64 `Mutex<HashMap<...>>` shards at `core_types.rs:265-350`; acquisitions go through `try_acquire()` at `core_types.rs:393-420`; finalize currently uses `lock_table.release_all(txn_id)` at `begin_concurrent.rs:1165-1168`, and `release_all()` scans every shard with `retain()` at `core_types.rs:464-478`.
   - Rough cost: medium/high structural cost. Per-page acquisition is at least one mutex; commit finalize does an O(all-shards + retained entries) cleanup instead of O(locks-held).
   - Candidate direction: carry the held-page list into prepared commit and switch finalize over to `release_set()` (`core_types.rs:480-495`), matching the cheaper lifecycle path already used at `lifecycle.rs:1352-1356`.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.3.3`, `bd-db300.2.3`.
5. `CommitIndex` sharded `RwLock` access on FCW validate + finalize publish.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/core_types.rs` + commit planner/finalizer.
   - Evidence: `CommitIndex` is 64 `RwLock<HashMap<...>>` shards at `core_types.rs:1275-1303`; FCW validation reads it in `begin_concurrent.rs:893-900`; finalize writes it per page at `begin_concurrent.rs:1165-1166`.
   - Rough cost: medium read-mostly choke point. Not obviously the top waiter by itself, but it compounds the global registry critical section and per-page finalize work.
   - Candidate direction: move toward read-mostly lock-free / sharded-atomic publication, or at minimum batch commit-index mutation outside wider registry-held work.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.2.3`, `bd-db300.3.2`, `bd-db300.5.3`.
6. SSI prepare/finalize scans active and committed history while registry is locked.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs`.
   - Evidence: prepare builds `views` / active refs and scans incoming+outgoing edges at `begin_concurrent.rs:942-968`; finalize rescans active state and mutates pivot metadata at `begin_concurrent.rs:1030-1144`.
   - Rough cost: medium/high structural cost that grows with active txn count and conflict history.
   - Candidate direction: snapshot active views outside the single registry mutex, reduce duplicate scans, and compact history so prepare/finalize are not O(active + history) under one guard.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.2.2`, `bd-db300.2.3`, `bd-db300.5.1`.
7. Pager WAL commit exclusive region still contains work that is not pure publication.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs`.
   - Evidence: `commit_wal()` escalates to `LockLevel::Exclusive` before frame vector assembly and WAL append/sync at `pager.rs:1237-1279`.
   - Rough cost: medium serialized region. Even if MVCC avoids SQLite-style writer locking at the semantic layer, the current file-backed path still wraps frame assembly + append + sync inside one exclusive region.
   - Candidate direction: push frame/checksum/serialization prep out of the exclusive window and batch durable append/group-commit work.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.3.1`, `bd-db300.3.2`, `bd-db300.3.3`, `bd-db300.3.5`.
8. E2E executor retry sleeps are benchmark-visible but not engine-internal.
   - Owner: `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs`.
   - Evidence: setup PRAGMA retries sleep at `fsqlite_executor.rs:162-176`; batch retries sleep at `fsqlite_executor.rs:545-556`; backoff helper is `fsqlite_executor.rs:822-828`.
   - Rough cost: secondary / measurement-layer wait. These sleeps amplify benchmark wall time once the engine returns `Busy` or `BusySnapshot`, but they are downstream of the engine choke points above.
   - Candidate direction: preserve separate accounting, then revisit retry policy after the engine-side blocking sites are reduced.
   - Follow-on beads: `bd-db300.2.4`, `bd-db300.7.1`.

Mode-specific note:
- Forced single-writer mode has an additional busy-wait drain loop in `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs:1450-1489` that sleeps 1ms while scanning `total_lock_count()`. This is relevant to Track H (`bd-db300.8.*`) but is not part of the ordinary MVCC path I ranked above.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyCliff","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:53:43.422007698Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:11.903411215Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T05:37:51.268883598Z","close_reason":"Completed: evidence-backed hot-path synchronization inventory posted in notes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["busy","concurrency","db300-program","db300-track-b","hot-path","locking","perf","profiling","track-b"]}
